  • Hide All Code

Quantum Bootstrap Phylogenomics Pipeline

J. M. A. Wojahn & P. E. Ellestad

2026-06-07

License

Released under the AGPL v. 3 license by Gnoll Pack Analytics, LLC.

Recommended Citation

Wojahn, J.M.A., Ellestad, P.E. (2026) Integrating Frequentist Bootstrapping with Quantum-Inspired Phylogenetic Reconstruction: A Proof-of-Concept. github.com/wojahn/quantum-bootstrap-phylogenomics DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20534276

Summary

This pipeline integrates a top-down quantum graph-splitting architecture into a frequentist resampling loop to produce statistical branch support values for quantum-inspired phylogenetic trees. Classical nonparametric resampling serves as the outer loop, while the quantum-inspired annealing engine handles tree reconstruction within each individual replicate — a design that makes the pipeline immediately executable on software-based simulators without physical quantum hardware.

As a proof-of-concept, the pipeline is applied to an empirical plastome dataset of 19 Pandanaceae taxa within a simulated quantum annealing environment. Validation proceeds through three complementary approaches:

  • Topological Mapping: Mapping quantum-inspired replicate topologies onto a fixed classical ML reference tree to calculate Quantum Bootstrap Support (QBS) values.
  • Cloudscape Density Visuals: Compiling the 100 quantum-derived topologies into a DensiTree cloudscape to visualize topological variance density.
  • Metric Convergence Audits: Calculating absolute Robinson-Foulds (RF) distances across the distribution to test whether the annealing backend converges on a biologically plausible, constrained neighborhood of treespace.

Pipeline Overview

Figure 1. Flowchart of the full pipeline from taxon selection through support mapping.

1. Software Installation

1.1 R Packages

Install all required CRAN, Bioconductor, and GitHub-hosted packages. Missing packages are detected and installed automatically.

# 1. Install CRAN-hosted core phylogenetics and infrastructure tools
cran_packages <- c("ape", "phangorn", "treeio", "TreeDist", "reticulate", "remotes")
new_cran <- cran_packages[!(cran_packages %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"])]

if (length(new_cran) > 0) {
  message("Installing missing CRAN packages: ", paste(new_cran, collapse = ", "))
  install.packages(new_cran, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
}

# 2. Install Bioconductor packages required for sequence alignment frameworks
bioc_packages <- c("DECIPHER", "Biostrings")
new_bioc <- bioc_packages[!(bioc_packages %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"])]

if (length(new_bioc) > 0) {
  message("Installing missing Bioconductor packages: ", paste(new_bioc, collapse = ", "))
  if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("BiocManager", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
  }
  BiocManager::install(new_bioc, update = FALSE, ask = FALSE)
}

# 3. Install GitHub-hosted custom tools
if (!requireNamespace("PrideBar", quietly = TRUE)) {
  message("Installing Custom Development Package: PrideBar from GitHub")
  remotes::install_github("wojahn/PrideBar", upgrade = "never")
}

1.2 External Software

Install IQ-TREE 2 for classical ML inference, the PhyloTree_ReConst quantum partitioning library from Fernández-Otero et al. (2026), and the D-Wave Ocean SDK for simulated or physical quantum annealing. If a D-Wave Leap API key is available, enter it during dwave setup.

# Install classical phylogenomic software
brew install iqtree2

# Install package from Fernández-Otero et al. (2026) 
git clone https://github.com/Nicofero/PhyloTree_ReConst.git
cd PhyloTree_ReConst
cp qaoa_functions.py qa_phylo_tree.ipynb qa_hybrid_trees.py qa_functions.py qa_big_trees.py ..
cd ..

# Install D-Wave simulator/quantum computer access
pip install numpy pandas networkx pyqubo dwave-ocean-sdk
dwave setup --auth
# Enter API key if you have one

# Install SeaView
conda install -c bioconda seaview

2. Reproducibility

Set a global random seed before any stochastic operations to ensure fully reproducible results across all bootstrap resampling and annealing steps.

set.seed(35753)

3. Taxon Selection and Sequence Alignment

Extract the 19-taxon cohort (16 Pandanus ingroup, 2 Benstonea outgroups, 1 Freycinetia root) from the master plastome file of Wojahn et al. (2023). The IRB inverted repeat is removed upstream in the source file. Sequences are re-aligned using a guide-tree profile-to-profile approach in DECIPHER, with gap smoothing applied after initial alignment construction.

library(ape)
library(DECIPHER)
library(Biostrings)

# This is the clean plastome file from Wojahn et al. (2023), Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution
plastomes <- ape::read.FASTA("AlignedOrderedGoodPlastomes_First.fasta")

# Extract the strategic 19-taxon cohort (16 Ingroup, 2 Benstonea, 1 Freycinetia)
plastomes <- plastomes[c(6, 8, 9, 44, 30, 51, 59, 13, 119, 63, 84, 17, 106, 68, 35, 114, 113, 43, 31)]

names(plastomes) <- c(
  "Freycinetia_baueriana", "Benstonea_copelandii",
  "Benstonea_herbacea", "Pandanus_furcatus", "Pandanus_calcis",
  "Pandanus_irregularis", "Pandanus_lacuum", "Pandanus_amaryllifolius",
  "Pandanus_vitiensis", "Pandanus_luzonensis", "Pandanus_polyglossus",
  "Pandanus_aquaticus", "Pandanus_tectorius", "Pandanus_maximus",
  "Pandanus_concretus", "Pandanus_utilis", "Pandanus_tsingycola",
  "Pandanus_eydouxia", "Pandanus_callmanderianus"
)

ape::write.FASTA(plastomes, "plastomes.fasta")

# Re-align raw profiles using DECIPHER framework
plastomez <- readDNAStringSet("plastomes.fasta")
plastomez <- DECIPHER::RemoveGaps(plastomez)
plastomez_a <- DECIPHER::AlignSeqs(plastomez, processors = 10)
plastomez_a <- DECIPHER::AdjustAlignment(plastomez_a)
Biostrings::writeXStringSet(plastomez_a, "Pandanus_a.fasta")

4. Classical Maximum Likelihood Reference Tree

Infer the ML reference topology in IQ-TREE 2 using ModelFinder Plus for automatic substitution model selection and 1,000 UFBoot replicates. The resulting tree is rooted on Freycinetia baueriana, ladderized, and saved as the fixed comparative backbone for all downstream QBS mapping.

iqtree_cmd <- "iqtree -s Pandanus_a.fasta -m MFP -bb 1000 -nt AUTO -redo -o Freycinetia_baueriana -seed 35753 -safe"
system(iqtree_cmd)

# Parse optimal classical configuration
classical_ref <- read.tree("Pandanus_a.fasta.treefile")
classical_ref <- root(classical_ref, "Freycinetia_baueriana", resolve.root = TRUE)
classical_ref <- drop.tip(classical_ref, "Freycinetia_baueriana")
classical_ref <- ladderize(classical_ref)

pdf("IQTREE_tree.pdf", width = 15, height = 20)
plot(classical_ref, show.node.label = FALSE, cex = 1.0, root.edge = TRUE)
nodelabels(classical_ref$node.label, adj = c(1.2, -1.5), frame = "none",
           cex = 0.8, font = 2, col = "darkblue")
dev.off()

5. Classical Frequentist Bootstrap Resampling

Generate 100 non-parametric bootstrap replicates via Felsenstein’s (1985) protocol. For each replicate, a starting topology is estimated from a Jukes-Cantor distance matrix using Neighbor-Joining, then fully optimized under GTR+\(\Gamma\) with Nearest-Neighbor Interchange rearrangements. Optimizing each replicate prior to matrix export removes bootstrap sampling noise before QUBO conversion. Each finalized tree is converted to a cophenetic distance matrix and written to disk.

library(phangorn)
library(PrideBar)

# Read in alignment again in correct format
final_alignment <- read.phyDat("Pandanus_a.fasta", format = "fasta")

# Perform Felsensteinian bootstrapping
boot_alignments <- bootstrap.phyDat(final_alignment, FUN = identity, bs = 100)
dir.create("bootstrap_inputs", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Make distance matrices
PrideBar::SetPrideBar(1, 100, 2)
for (i in 1:100) {
  PrideBar::PrideBar()
  rep_data <- boot_alignments[[i]]

  # Generate initial fast structural layout
  tmp_dm   <- dist.ml(rep_data, model = "JC69")
  tmp_tree <- NJ(tmp_dm)
  tmp_fit  <- pml(tmp_tree, data = rep_data)

  # Optimize parameters via NNI to fix bootstrap sampling noise before matrix output
  suppressMessages(
    tmp_opt <- optim.pml(tmp_fit, model = "GTR", k = 4,
                         optEdge = TRUE, optBf = TRUE, optQ = TRUE, optGamma = TRUE,
                         rearrangement = "NNI", control = pml.control(trace = 0))
  )

  # Extract the clean, mathematically validated hierarchical matrix
  rep_matrix <- as.matrix(cophenetic(tmp_opt$tree))

  # Save the finalized matrix
  file_name <- paste0("bootstrap_inputs/matrix_", sprintf("%03d", i), ".csv")
  write.csv(rep_matrix, file = file_name, row.names = TRUE)
}
PrideBar::FadePrideBar()

6. Quantum and Quantum-Inspired Bootstrap Tree Reconstruction

Each cophenetic distance matrix is converted into an edge-weighted similarity graph and partitioned recursively via the Normalized Minimum Cut criterion, mapped into QUBO form using the PhyloTree_ReConst API. Two execution backends are provided: a physical D-Wave QPU (Section 6.1) and a local classical simulated annealing simulator (Section 6.2). Both write 100 resolved Newick topologies to a master output file.

6.1 Physical Quantum Annealing (D-Wave QPU)

Use this version if a D-Wave Leap API key is configured and access to a Pegasus-topology QPU is available.

# REAL Quantum version
dwave_python_script_content <- c(
  "import os",
  "import sys",
  "import glob",
  "import pandas as pd",
  "import numpy as np",
  "import qa_functions as qaf",
  "from dwave.system import DWaveSampler, EmbeddingComposite",
  "",
  "matrix_dir = 'bootstrap_inputs' if os.path.exists('bootstrap_inputs') else '../bootstrap_inputs'",
  "matrix_files = sorted(glob.glob(f'{matrix_dir}/matrix_*.csv'))",
  "",
  "if not matrix_files:",
  "    print(f\"Error: Cannot find matrix files in '{matrix_dir}'. Execution halted.\")",
  "    sys.exit(1)",
  "",
  "print(f\"Connecting to D-Wave Live QPU Hardware via Leap...\")",
  "try:",
  "    qpu_sampler = DWaveSampler(solver={'topology__type': 'pegasus'})",
  "    qpu_backend = EmbeddingComposite(qpu_sampler)",
  "    print(f\" Connected successfully to: {qpu_sampler.properties['id']}\")",
  "except Exception as e:",
  "    print(f\"Hardware Connection Error: {e}\")",
  "    print(\"Ensure your D-Wave API token is configured via environment variables or 'dwave config'.\")",
  "    sys.exit(1)",
  "",
  "print(f\"Found {len(matrix_files)} bootstrap matrices. Resolving on physical QPU...\")",
  "bootstrap_topologies = []",
  "",
  "for i, file_path in enumerate(matrix_files, 1):",
  "    df = pd.read_csv(file_path, index_col=0)",
  "    matrix = df.to_numpy()",
  "    taxa_labels = list(df.index)",
  "",
  "    max_dist = np.max(matrix) if np.max(matrix) > 0 else 1.0",
  "    similarity_matrix = max_dist - matrix",
  "    np.fill_diagonal(similarity_matrix, 0)",
  "",
  "    int_tags = list(range(len(taxa_labels)))",
  "    ",
  "    native_tree_object = qaf.sa_phylo_tree(",
  "        similarity_matrix, ",
  "        tags=int_tags, ",
  "        sampler=qpu_backend, ",
  "        num_reads=1000, ",
  "        annealing_time=20",
  "    )",
  "",
  "    newick_str = native_tree_object.to_newick(labels=taxa_labels)",
  "    bootstrap_topologies.append(newick_str)",
  "",
  "    if i % 5 == 0:",
  "        print(f\"  -> Processed {i}/100 profiles directly on the QPU.\")",
  "",
  "output_file = 'quantum_bootstrap_trees.tre'",
  "with open(output_file, 'w') as f:",
  "    for tree in bootstrap_topologies:",
  "        f.write(f\"{tree}\\n\")",
  "",
  "print(f\"\\n 100 QPU-annealed topologies populated and saved to '{output_file}'.\")"
)

writeLines(dwave_python_script_content, con = "run_quantum_bootstrap.py")

6.2 Simulated Quantum Annealing (Classical Simulator)

Use this version for execution on standard hardware without QPU access. The neal SimulatedAnnealingSampler mirrors the minimization logic of a physical Pegasus topology, running 1,000 thermodynamic Monte Carlo trajectories per graph-cut calculation.

library(reticulate)

# SIMULATED Quantum version
dwave_python_script_content <- c(
  "import os",
  "import sys",
  "import glob",
  "import pandas as pd",
  "import numpy as np",
  "import qa_functions as qaf",
  "import neal",
  "",
  "# Verify paths across platforms",
  "matrix_dir = 'bootstrap_inputs' if os.path.exists('bootstrap_inputs') else '../bootstrap_inputs'",
  "matrix_files = sorted(glob.glob(f'{matrix_dir}/matrix_*.csv'))",
  "",
  "if not matrix_files:",
  "    print(f\"Error: Cannot find matrix files in '{matrix_dir}'. Execution halted.\")",
  "    sys.exit(1)",
  "",
  "print(\"Initializing classical Simulated Annealing simulator backend via neal...\")",
  "try:",
  "    # Initialize the local CPU simulator",
  "    sim_backend = neal.SimulatedAnnealingSampler()",
  "    print(\" Classical simulation environment established successfully.\")",
  "except Exception as e:",
  "    print(f\"Backend Initialization Error: {e}\")",
  "    sys.exit(1)",
  "",
  "print(f\"Found {len(matrix_files)} bootstrap matrices. Resolving via local heuristic sweeps...\")",
  "bootstrap_topologies = []",
  "",
  "for i, file_path in enumerate(matrix_files, 1):",
  "    df = pd.read_csv(file_path, index_col=0)",
  "    matrix = df.to_numpy()",
  "    taxa_labels = list(df.index)",
  "",
  "    # Invert matrix to build similarity edge arrays",
  "    max_dist = np.max(matrix) if np.max(matrix) > 0 else 1.0",
  "    similarity_matrix = max_dist - matrix",
  "    np.fill_diagonal(similarity_matrix, 0)",
  "",
  "    # Direct recursive top-down graph partition mapping",
  "    int_tags = list(range(len(taxa_labels)))",
  "    ",
  "    # Pass the local simulator engine along with sample read overrides",
  "    # num_reads=1000: 1000 thermodynamic Monte Carlo trajectories per split",
  "    native_tree_object = qaf.sa_phylo_tree(",
  "        similarity_matrix, ",
  "        tags=int_tags, ",
  "        sampler=sim_backend, ",
  "        num_reads=1000",
  "    )",
  "",
  "    # Convert structural tree object to labels-aware Newick text",
  "    newick_str = native_tree_object.to_newick(labels=taxa_labels)",
  "    bootstrap_topologies.append(newick_str)",
  "",
  "    if i % 5 == 0:",
  "        print(f\"  -> Processed {i}/100 profiles using local simulated annealing.\")",
  "",
  "# Save simulation payloads",
  "output_file = 'quantum_inspired_bootstrap_trees.tre'",
  "with open(output_file, 'w') as f:",
  "    for tree in bootstrap_topologies:",
  "        f.write(f\"{tree}\\n\")",
  "",
  "print(f\"\\n 100 simulated annealing topologies populated and saved to '{output_file}'.\")"
)

writeLines(dwave_python_script_content, "run_quantum_bootstrap.py")

# Call Python backend via reticulate
use_condaenv("base", required = TRUE)
py_run_file("run_quantum_bootstrap.py")

7. Bootstrap Tree Ingestion and Resolution Audit

Read the 100 quantum bootstrap topologies from disk, sanitize tip labels for consistency with the ML reference backbone, and drop any rogue taxa introduced by string artifacts from the Python parser. Tree resolution is computed across all replicates as the ratio of resolved internal nodes to the maximum possible unrooted splits for a 19-taxon tree.

# Read in classical tree
target_tree_file  <- "Pandanus_a.fasta.treefile"

# Read in quantum bootstrap trees
quantum_boot_file <- "quantum_bootstrap_trees.tre"

raw_ref <- ape::read.tree(target_tree_file)

# Ensure no weird string artifacts from python
raw_ref$tip.label <- chartr("-", "_", raw_ref$tip.label)
raw_ref$tip.label <- trimws(raw_ref$tip.label)
ref_labels <- sort(raw_ref$tip.label)

raw_ref_unrooted <- ape::unroot(raw_ref)

# Calculate maximum possible number of splits
max_possible_splits <- length(raw_ref_unrooted$tip.label) - 3

# Read in bootstrap trees
boot_lines <- readLines(quantum_boot_file)
boot_lines <- boot_lines[boot_lines != ""]

clean_boot_list <- lapply(1:length(boot_lines), function(i) {
  tr <- tryCatch(ape::read.tree(text = boot_lines[i]), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(tr)) {
    tr$tip.label <- chartr("-", "_", tr$tip.label)
    tr$tip.label <- trimws(tr$tip.label)
    rogue_taxa <- tr$tip.label[!tr$tip.label %in% ref_labels]
    if (length(rogue_taxa) > 0) {
      tr <- ape::drop.tip(tr, rogue_taxa)
    }
  }
  return(tr)
})
clean_boot_list <- clean_boot_list[!sapply(clean_boot_list, is.null)]
class(clean_boot_list) <- "multiPhylo"

# Compute resolution metrics
resolution_percentages <- sapply(1:length(clean_boot_list), function(i) {
  boot_tree    <- ape::unroot(clean_boot_list[[i]])
  actual_splits <- boot_tree$Nnode
  if (actual_splits > max_possible_splits) actual_splits <- max_possible_splits
  return((actual_splits / max_possible_splits) * 100)
})

message("\n=== QUANTUM BOOTSTRAP RESOLUTION AUDIT ===")
print(summary(resolution_percentages))

8. Quantum Bootstrap Support Mapping

Map bipartition frequencies from the 100 annealing topologies onto the fixed ML reference backbone using phangorn::plotBS to calculate QBS values at each internal node. Freycinetia baueriana is dropped after support mapping to isolate the 18-taxon ingroup for visualization. The hybrid QBS tree is exported as a PDF.

# Plot classical topology with bootstrap support
supported_tree <- phangorn::plotBS(raw_ref, clean_boot_list, type = "none", p = 0)

# Drop outgroup to focus on ingroup support matrix visualization
supported_tree <- ape::root(supported_tree, "Freycinetia_baueriana", resolve.root = TRUE)
supported_tree <- ape::drop.tip(supported_tree, "Freycinetia_baueriana")
supported_tree <- ape::ladderize(supported_tree)
qbs_values <- supported_tree$node.label

# Render primary hybrid support tree
pdf("quantum_bootstrap_hybrid_tree.pdf", width = 12, height = 15)
par(mar = c(5, 8, 4, 2))
ape::plot.phylo(supported_tree, cex = 1.0, font = 3, edge.width = 2, label.offset = 0.0001)
ape::nodelabels(
  text = paste0(as.character(as.numeric(qbs_values) * 100),
                ifelse(qbs_values == "", "", "%")),
  adj = c(1, -0.5), frame = "none", cex = 0.8, font = 2, col = "darkblue"
)
dev.off()

9. DensiTree Cloudogram Visualization

Root and bifurcate all 100 bootstrap topologies on Freycinetia baueriana, assign unit branch lengths for cladogram display, and render a DensiTree cloudscape overlaid on the classical ML consensus backbone. The cloudogram visualizes the full distribution of topological variance across the bootstrap replicates.

# Keep all 19 taxa intact (do not drop Freycinetia_baueriana)
rooted_boot_list <- lapply(1:length(clean_boot_list), function(i) {
  tr <- clean_boot_list[[i]]

  # Root stably on the absolute outgroup tip across all 100 replicates
  tr_rooted    <- ape::root(tr, outgroup = "Freycinetia_baueriana", resolve.root = TRUE)
  tr_bifurcated <- ape::multi2di(tr_rooted)
  tr_bifurcated$edge.length <- rep(1, nrow(tr_bifurcated$edge))
  tr_clean <- ape::ladderize(ape::read.tree(text = ape::write.tree(tr_bifurcated)))
  return(tr_clean)
})
class(rooted_boot_list) <- "multiPhylo"

# Re-ingest the reference tree from Phase 6 but KEEP Freycinetia for the background backbone
classical_ref_rooted <- phangorn::plotBS(raw_ref, clean_boot_list, type = "none", p = 0)
classical_ref_rooted <- ape::root(classical_ref_rooted, outgroup = "Freycinetia_baueriana", resolve.root = TRUE)
classical_ref_rooted <- ape::multi2di(classical_ref_rooted)
classical_ref_rooted$edge.length <- rep(1, nrow(classical_ref_rooted$edge))

pdf("Quantum_Bootstrap_Densitree.pdf", width = 12, height = 8)
par(mar = c(3, 3, 5, 10))
phangorn::densiTree(
  rooted_boot_list,
  type      = "cladogram",
  alpha     = 0.15,
  col       = "royalblue",
  consensus = classical_ref_rooted,
  direction = "rightwards",
  scaleX    = TRUE,
  cex       = 0.9,
  font      = 3,
  main      = "Quantum Bootstrap Densitree\n(Topological Variation Cloud vs. Classical Baseline)"
)
dev.off()

10. Robinson-Foulds Distance Profile

Calculate absolute RF distances between the ML reference backbone and each of the 100 quantum bootstrap replicates. The resulting distribution is summarized and rendered as a histogram with the median RF distance marked. Values well below the random expectation threshold confirm that the annealing engine explores a constrained, biologically meaningful neighborhood of treespace.

library(TreeDist)

# Calculate pairwise RF distances between the classical tree and quantum bootstrap trees
rf_distances <- sapply(1:length(clean_boot_list), function(i) {
  return(TreeDist::RobinsonFoulds(supported_tree, clean_boot_list[[i]]))
})

message("\n=== ROBINSON-FOULDS DISTANCE AUDIT ===")
print(summary(rf_distances))

# Make histogram
pdf("Quantum_Bootstrap_RF_Histogram.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
max_rf <- max(rf_distances, na.rm = TRUE)
if (max_rf == 0) max_rf <- 2
hist_breaks <- seq(-0.5, max_rf + 0.5, by = 1)

hist(rf_distances, breaks = hist_breaks, col = "skyblue", border = "white",
     main = "Topological Divergence Profile\n(Quantum Bootstrap Cloud vs. Classical Baseline)",
     xlab = "Absolute Robinson-Foulds (RF) Distance",
     ylab = "Frequency (Number of Replicates)",
     xaxt = "n", las = 1)
axis(1, at = 0:max_rf, labels = 0:max_rf)
abline(v = median(rf_distances), col = "darkred", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
legend("topright",
       legend = paste("Median RF Distance =", median(rf_distances)),
       col = "darkred", lwd = 2, lty = 2, box.col = "white")
dev.off()

11. Summary Metrics and Node Support Tables

Compile resolution, RF distance, and QBS statistics into a structured summary table and per-node support table. Both are exported as CSV files for downstream reporting. The final block calculates the median and IQR of the per-node QBS vs. UFBoot difference.

# Summarize metrics
res_summary     <- summary(resolution_percentages)
rf_summary      <- summary(rf_distances)
raw_qbs_numeric <- as.numeric(qbs_values)
clean_qbs_pct   <- raw_qbs_numeric[!is.na(raw_qbs_numeric)] * 100
if (length(clean_qbs_pct) == 0) clean_qbs_pct <- 0

dataset_summary_matrix <- data.frame(
  Metric_Category = c(
    "Topological Resolution (%)", "Topological Resolution (%)", "Topological Resolution (%)",
    "Robinson-Foulds (RF) Distance", "Robinson-Foulds (RF) Distance", "Robinson-Foulds (RF) Distance",
    "Quantum Bootstrap Support (QBS)", "Quantum Bootstrap Support (QBS)", "Quantum Bootstrap Support (QBS)"
  ),
  Statistical_Parameter = c(
    "Minimum", "Median", "Maximum",
    "Minimum", "Median", "Maximum",
    "Minimum Node Support", "Median Node Support", "Maximum Node Support"
  ),
  Value = c(
    round(res_summary[["Min."]], 2),    round(res_summary[["Median"]], 2),    round(res_summary[["Max."]], 2),
    round(rf_summary[["Min."]], 2),     round(rf_summary[["Median"]], 2),     round(rf_summary[["Max."]], 2),
    paste0(round(min(clean_qbs_pct), 1), "%"),
    paste0(round(median(clean_qbs_pct), 1), "%"),
    paste0(round(max(clean_qbs_pct), 1), "%")
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

n_tips            <- length(supported_tree$tip.label)
internal_node_ids <- (n_tips + 1):(n_tips + supported_tree$Nnode)
formatted_pct_column <- sapply(raw_qbs_numeric, function(val) {
  if (is.na(val)) return("Root/Unresolved Split")
  return(paste0(round(val * 100, 1), "%"))
})

node_support_table <- data.frame(
  Plot_Node_ID           = internal_node_ids,
  Raw_Support_Proportion = supported_tree$node.label,
  Formatted_QBS_Percentage = formatted_pct_column,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# QBS vs UFBoot difference summary
uwu <- c(as.numeric(qbs_values) * 100)[-1] - as.numeric(raw_ref$node.label)[-1]
summary(uwu)
paste0(round(median(uwu), 2), " \u00b1 ", round(IQR(uwu), 2))

print(dataset_summary_matrix, row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dataset_summary_matrix, file = "Quantum_Bootstrap_Dataset_Summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(node_support_table,     file = "Quantum_Bootstrap_Node_Support_Details.csv", row.names = FALSE)

12. Nodal Depth Analysis of Low-Support Conflict

Normalize root-to-tip depths for all internal nodes and identify those falling below the 70% QBS threshold. The median relative depth of these weak nodes is reported and automatically interpreted as root-proximal, mid-phylogeny, or tip-proximal conflict, characterizing the spatial distribution of topological uncertainty across the tree.

# Calculate all node depths
all_node_depths  <- ape::node.depth.edgelength(supported_tree)
internal_depths  <- all_node_depths[(n_tips + 1):length(all_node_depths)]
norm_depths      <- (internal_depths - min(internal_depths)) / (max(internal_depths) - min(internal_depths))

# Make output dataframe
support_depth_df <- data.frame(
  Node_ID       = (n_tips + 1):length(all_node_depths),
  QBS           = as.numeric(supported_tree$node.label) * 100,
  Relative_Depth = norm_depths
)

# Define and extract weak nodes
weak_nodes <- support_depth_df[!is.na(support_depth_df$QBS) & support_depth_df$QBS < 70, ]

# Report metrics
if (nrow(weak_nodes) > 0) {
  median_weak_depth <- median(weak_nodes$Relative_Depth)
  message("\n=== LOW-SUPPORT MIDDLENESS AUDIT ===")
  message("Number of low-support nodes (<70%): ", nrow(weak_nodes))
  message("Median Relative Depth of weak nodes: ", round(median_weak_depth, 4))

  if (median_weak_depth > 0.4 && median_weak_depth < 0.6) {
    message("Interpretation: Weak support is heavily clustered in the MID-SECTION of the tree topology.")
  } else if (median_weak_depth <= 0.4) {
    message("Interpretation: Weak support is clustered deep near the ROOT base (uwu).")
  } else {
    message("Interpretation: Weak support is clustered out near the peripheral TIPS.")
  }
} else {
  message("All nodes possess high support; no topological weak zones detected.")
}

13. Classical Distance Bootstrap Reference

Read in all 100 pre-optimized cophenetic matrices and reconstruct NJ topologies directly, providing a classical distance-matrix comparator that isolates the quantum annealing step as the sole variable between pipelines.

matrix_files <- list.files("bootstrap_inputs", pattern = "matrix_.*\\.csv", full.names = TRUE)
matrix_files <- sort(matrix_files)

classical_dist_boot_list <- lapply(1:length(matrix_files), function(i) {
  mat <- as.dist(read.csv(matrix_files[[i]], row.names = 1))
  tr  <- ape::nj(mat)
  return(tr)
})
class(classical_dist_boot_list) <- "multiPhylo"

# Map bipartition frequencies onto the ML backbone to get CDBS values
cdbs_tree <- phangorn::plotBS(raw_ref, classical_dist_boot_list, type = "none", p = 0)
cdbs_tree <- ape::root(cdbs_tree, "Freycinetia_baueriana", resolve.root = TRUE)
cdbs_tree <- ape::drop.tip(cdbs_tree, "Freycinetia_baueriana")
cdbs_tree <- ape::ladderize(cdbs_tree)

# Calculate RF distances against the unrooted reference with all 19 taxa intact
rf_distances_cdbs <- sapply(1:length(classical_dist_boot_list), function(i) {
  return(TreeDist::RobinsonFoulds(raw_ref_unrooted, classical_dist_boot_list[[i]]))
})

message("\n=== CLASSICAL DISTANCE BOOTSTRAP RESOLUTION AUDIT ===")
print(summary(sapply(classical_dist_boot_list, function(tr) {
  ape::unroot(tr)$Nnode / max_possible_splits * 100
})))

message("\n=== CLASSICAL DISTANCE BOOTSTRAP RF DISTANCES ===")
print(summary(rf_distances_cdbs))

message("\n=== CLASSICAL DISTANCE BOOTSTRAP SUPPORT (CDBS) ===")
cdbs_raw <- as.numeric(cdbs_tree$node.label) * 100
print(summary(cdbs_raw[!is.na(cdbs_raw)]))

# Node-by-node QBS vs CDBS comparison
qbs_numeric  <- as.numeric(qbs_values) * 100
cdbs_numeric <- cdbs_raw

# Trim to shared node count to guard against root node discrepancies
min_nodes    <- min(length(qbs_numeric), length(cdbs_numeric))
qbs_numeric  <- qbs_numeric[1:min_nodes]
cdbs_numeric <- cdbs_numeric[1:min_nodes]

delta_support <- qbs_numeric - cdbs_numeric

message("\n=== QBS vs CDBS NODE-BY-NODE DELTA ===")
print(summary(delta_support[!is.na(delta_support)]))
message("Median delta: ", paste0(round(median(delta_support, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
        " \u00b1 ", round(IQR(delta_support, na.rm = TRUE), 2)))

# Render side-by-side comparison plot
pdf("QBS_vs_CDBS_Comparison.pdf", width = 14, height = 15)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 8, 4, 2))

ape::plot.phylo(supported_tree, cex = 1.0, font = 3, edge.width = 2,
                label.offset = 0.0001, main = "Quantum Bootstrap Support (QBS)")
ape::nodelabels(
  text = paste0(as.character(round(qbs_numeric, 1)),
                ifelse(is.na(qbs_numeric), "", "%")),
  adj = c(1, -0.5), frame = "none", cex = 0.8, font = 2, col = "darkblue"
)

ape::plot.phylo(cdbs_tree, cex = 1.0, font = 3, edge.width = 2,
                label.offset = 0.0001, main = "Classical Distance Bootstrap Support (CDBS)")
ape::nodelabels(
  text = paste0(as.character(round(cdbs_numeric, 1)),
                ifelse(is.na(cdbs_numeric), "", "%")),
  adj = c(1, -0.5), frame = "none", cex = 0.8, font = 2, col = "darkred"
)
dev.off()

pdf("NJ_tree.pdf", width = 12, height = 15)
par(mar = c(5, 8, 4, 2))
ape::plot.phylo(cdbs_tree, cex = 1.0, font = 3, edge.width = 2,
                label.offset = 0.0001, main = "NJ Support (CDBS)")
ape::nodelabels(
  text = paste0(as.character(round(cdbs_numeric, 1)),
                ifelse(is.na(cdbs_numeric), "", "%")),
  adj = c(1, -0.5), frame = "none", cex = 0.8, font = 2, col = "darkblue"
)
dev.off()

# Scatterplot of QBS vs CDBS per node
pdf("QBS_vs_CDBS_Scatterplot.pdf", width = 7, height = 7)
valid_idx <- !is.na(qbs_numeric) & !is.na(cdbs_numeric)
plot(cdbs_numeric[valid_idx], qbs_numeric[valid_idx],
     xlab = "Classical Distance Bootstrap Support (CDBS %)",
     ylab = "Quantum Bootstrap Support (QBS %)",
     main = "QBS vs. CDBS Node Support",
     pch = 19, col = "steelblue", xlim = c(0, 100), ylim = c(0, 100))
abline(0, 1, col = "darkred", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
legend("topleft", legend = "1:1 line", col = "darkred", lty = 2,
       lwd = 2, box.col = "white")
dev.off()
